title: colchicaceae
intro: The Colchicaceae rank, also known as the Autumn-crocus family, refers to a family of flowering plants. This family consists of approximately 80 to 100 species and is characterized by its unique, often autumn-blooming flowers. The Colchicaceae family is primarily found in the Northern Hemisphere, with a few species native to the mountainous regions of Southern Hemisphere. The plants within this family are often low-growing, with leaves that are often strap-like or linear in shape.
